seo.title: San Juan VS Competitors | Maxima Pools - Columbus, OH
seo.description: See how San Juan's hand-laid fiberglass construction compares to chopped/sprayed competitors. 32-mil gel coat, Vinyl Ester resin throughout, and 5+ guaranteed layers.
seo.ogTitle: San Juan VS Competitors | Maxima Pools
seo.ogDescription: Hand-laid vs chopped fiberglass — see the 10-point comparison that proves San Juan builds the strongest pool on the market.

hero.subtitle: One simple truth drives everything we do: building the highest quality fiberglass pools in the world. See how San Juan's hand-laid process compares — point by point.

advantagesSection.intro: Nearly 70 years of hand-laid craftsmanship. The first fiberglass pool San Juan ever built is still in the ground today.

advantages.title: 100% Hand-Laid Sheets
advantages.description: Multiple layers of solid fiberglass sheets applied sequentially — replicating the same boat hull manufacturing process used in marine applications.

advantages.title: Vinyl Ester Throughout
advantages.description: Marine-grade Vinyl Ester resin applied throughout the entire pool shell — not just the last layer. Both sides are fully protected against water intrusion.

advantages.title: 32-mil Gel Coat
advantages.description: The thickest gel coat in the industry — two 16-mil layers for superior UV protection, stain resistance, and lasting color vibrancy.

advantages.title: No Coring or Fillers
advantages.description: Pure fiberglass composite throughout. No coring, no fillers, no chopped fiberglass, and no safety ledges needed — the shell is strong enough without them.

comparisonSection.intro: 10 manufacturing features compared. See exactly where San Juan outperforms every competitor.

rows.sanJuan: 100% hand-laid solid sheets with consistent thickness
rows.competitor: Chopped fiberglass sprayed from gun — inconsistent thickness

rows.sanJuan: Removed from each layer for optimal strength-to-weight ratio
rows.competitor: Cannot be removed — weakens the shell over time

rows.sanJuan: Applied throughout — significant vertical and horizontal strength
rows.competitor: Used only in limited areas — structural gaps remain

rows.sanJuan: Not used — zero chopped fiberglass in any San Juan pool
rows.competitor: 2" pieces sprayed on with variable thickness throughout

rows.sanJuan: Not needed — shell is strong enough without them
rows.competitor: Required for stiffening — collects dirt, potential hazard

rows.sanJuan: Not used — pure fiberglass composite throughout
rows.competitor: Present in many models — increases brittleness risk

rows.sanJuan: Applied throughout the entire pool shell — both sides
32-mil thickness — two 16-mil layers (thickest in the industry)
Guaranteed minimum 5 hand-laid fiberglass layers per shell
rows.competitor: No guaranteed minimum — varies by model and manufacturer

rows.sanJuan: Factory-manufactured with controlled QA at every step
rows.competitor: Variable quality — depends on spray operator skill

handlaid.title: San Juan — Hand-Laid
handlaid.subtitle: The right way
benefits: Guaranteed minimum 5 hand-laid fiberglass layers per shell
Consistent thickness throughout the entire mold — front to back
Excess resin removed from each layer for optimal strength-to-weight ratio
No safety ledges needed — shell integrity stands on its own
Vinyl Ester resin throughout entire pool — both sides protected
32-mil gel coat in two layers — thickest and most durable in the industry

chopped.title: Competitors — Chopped
chopped.subtitle: Cutting corners
downsides: No consistency in fiberglass thickness — depends on spray gun duration
Resin concentrates in random areas, creating uneven weak layers
Requires interior "safety ledges" that weaken the shell and collect dirt
Variable quality depends on spray operator skill, not controlled QA
Vinyl Ester resin used only on the last layer — interior unprotected
Thinner gel coat (27–30 mil) with single-layer application

evidenceSection.paragraph: These spec sheets show the structural differences between San Juan's hand-laid construction and competitors' chopped methods. Click to zoom in and see the details.
